name: Roast Turkey with Stuffings
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the turkey neck and giblets from the cavities of the bird. Rinse the turkey and wipe it dry. (Cook the neck and giblets for broth or to make giblet gravy.) Prepare the stuffing of your choice.
2. Stuff loosely into the neck cavity, then skewer the neck skin to the back.
3. Stuff the body cavity loosely.
4. Sew any openings shut by lacing a piece of string on wood picks. Fasten down the legs either by tying or tucking under the skin band. Twist the wings inward.
5. Place the turkey, breast up, on a rack in a shallow roasting pan. Brush with melted butter.
6. If using a roast meat thermometer, insert it into the thick part of the thigh. The bulb should not touch the bone.
7. Roast at 325°F. A tent of foil placed loosely over the turkey keeps it from browning too fast and may be removed when necessary to baste the turkey.
8. Remove the foil last for about half an hour for final browning.
9. The turkey is done when the thermometer registers 180 to 185°F, or when the thick part of the drumstick feels tender when pressed with your thumb and forefinger, or when the drumstick and thigh move easily.
